explanation: This bypasses the rule because the Sigma logic explicitly looks for the execution of 'reg.exe' (selection_reg). By modifying the registry key using PowerShell's built-in Set-ItemProperty cmdlet, the process name is 'powershell.exe', causing the rule's 'all of' condition to fail.

This bypasses the rule by utilizing command-line obfuscation with the caret (^) character, which is ignored by the Windows command interpreter. The rule checks for the exact string '\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' (selection_path). The insertion of the caret breaks this string matching, evading detection while still successfully modifying the registry.
